heading: Authority of Chief of Engineers
label: 9502 Authority of Chief of Engineers

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Chief of Engineers and necessary assistants may use all lawful means to carry out their duties.

children.heading: Supply of Water in District of Columbia.—
children.label: b Supply of Water in District of Columbia.—

children.heading: .—Providing water
children.label: 1 .—Providing water
children.text: The Chief of Engineers has complete control over the Washington Aqueduct to regulate the manner in which the authorities of the District of Columbia may tap the supply of water to the inhabitants of the District of Columbia.

children.heading: .—Stoppage of water flow
children.label: 2 .—Stoppage of water flow
children.text: The Chief of Engineers shall stop the authorities of the District of Columbia from tapping the supply of water when the supply is no more than adequate to the wants of the public buildings and grounds.

children.heading: .—Appeal of decision
children.label: 3 .—Appeal of decision
children.text: The decision of the Chief of Engineers on all questions concerning the supply of water under this subsection may be appealed only to the Secretary of the Army.
